data.title: Gentex reports Q1 2026: Revenue $675.4M, Net Income $98.5M, Adjusted EPS $0.48
data.description:
Gentex reported consolidated net sales of $675.4 million for the quarter ended March 31, 2026, with net income attributable to Gentex of $98.5 million and adjusted diluted earnings per share of $0.48. Core Gentex (ex-VOXX) revenue was $586.8 million and consolidated gross margin was 33.8% for the quarter. The company repurchased 3.3 million shares for $71.6 million during the quarter and updated full-year 2026 revenue guidance to $2.65–$2.75 billion.

Financial Highlights

  • Consolidated net sales: $675.4 million for Q1 2026; core Gentex (ex-VOXX) net sales: $586.8 million.
  • Gross profit: Consolidated gross margin 33.8%; core Gentex gross margin 34.0% for Q1 2026.
  • Income from operations: Consolidated (GAAP) $123.7 million; core Gentex income from operations $117.9 million; adjusted core Gentex income from operations (non-GAAP) $121.4 million.
  • Net income attributable to Gentex (GAAP): $98.5 million; adjusted net income (non-GAAP): $103.7 million.
  • Adjusted diluted earnings per share (non-GAAP): $0.48; GAAP diluted earnings per share: $0.46. Share repurchases: 3.3 million shares for $71.6 million.

Business Highlights

  • VOXX integration: VOXX contributed $88.6 million of revenue in Q1 and achieved profitability one year after acquisition; focus on scaling product launches and expanding sales channels.
  • Product mix and feature penetration drove core growth: North American revenue rose ~6% year-over-year driven by Full Display Mirror (FDM) penetration despite regional light vehicle production declines.
  • Regional performance: International mirror unit shipments declined (notably an ~8% decline in Europe, Japan and Korea unit shipments), but revenue decline in those regions was limited by favorable product mix and ramp of an ICMS platform.
  • Other product lines growth: Gentex Other (aircraft dimmable windows, fire protection, medical devices, biometrics) revenue increased ~60% year-over-year, with gains in aircraft windows, fire protection and biometrics.
  • Tariffs and supply pressures: Ongoing tariff and commodity cost headwinds (including IEEPA-related tariffs) have been reflected in outlook; approximately $15 million of tariff costs capitalized in inventory as of March 31, 2026.

Original SEC Filing: GENTEX CORP [ GNTX ] - 8-K - Apr. 24, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Moog Q2 sales rise more than expected; co raises full-year EPS outlook
data.description:
Overview

  • US precision controls maker's fiscal Q2 sales rose 13%, beating analyst expectations

  • Adjusted EPS for fiscal Q2 beat consensus, up 40% year-over-year

  • Company raised full-year adjusted EPS guidance, citing strong demand and margin gains

Outlook

  • Moog raises FY 2026 adjusted diluted EPS guidance to $10.60, from $10.20 previously

  • Company maintains FY 2026 net sales forecast at $4.3 bln and adjusted operating margin at 13.4%

  • Moog expects stronger business performance in second half of 2026

Result Drivers

  • SEGMENT GROWTH - Co said sales increased across all four segments, with Space and Defense up 16% on broad-based defense demand and Commercial Aircraft up 15% on higher volume and pricing

  • MARGIN IMPROVEMENT - Higher operating margin attributed to profitable sales growth, pricing and operational performance, partially offset by tariff pressure

  • CASH FLOW GAINS - Free cash flow rose sharply, driven by strong earnings and working capital management

data.title: Sierra Bancorp Declares Quarterly Cash Dividend
data.description:
PORTERVILLE, Calif.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--April 24, 2026--

Sierra Bancorp , parent of Bank of the Sierra, announced that its Board of Directors has declared a regular quarterly cash dividend of $0.26 per share. The dividend was approved subsequent to the Board's review of the Company's financial performance and capital for the quarter ended March 31, 2026, and will be paid on May 11, 2026, to shareholders of record as of May 4, 2026. Counting dividends paid by Bank of the Sierra prior to the formation of Sierra Bancorp, the Company has paid regular cash dividends to shareholders every year since 1987, comprised of annual dividends through 1998 and quarterly dividends thereafter. The dividend noted in today's announcement marks the Company's 109(th) consecutive quarterly cash dividend.

Sierra Bancorp is the holding Company for Bank of the Sierra (www.bankofthesierra.com), which is in its 49th year of operations and strives to be the preeminent bank headquartered in the South San Joaquin Valley. Bank of the Sierra offers a broad range of retail and commercial banking services through its 34 full-service branches located within the counties of Tulare, Kern, Kings, Fresno, Ventura, San Luis Obispo, and Santa Barbara. The Bank also maintains an online branch and provides specialized lending services through its mortgage warehouse division. In 2025, Bank of the Sierra was recognized as one of the strongest and top-performing community banks in the country, with a 5-star rating from Bauer Financial.

Forward-Looking Statements

The statements contained in this release that are not historical facts are forward-looking statements based on management's current expectations and beliefs concerning future deSHYvelopments and their potential effects on the Company. Readers are cautioned not to unduly rely on forward looking statements. Actual results may differ from those projected. These forward-looking statements involve risks and uncertainties including but not limited to the health of the national and local economies, loan portfolio performance, the Company's ability to attract and retain skilled employees, customers' service expectations, the Company's ability to successfully deSHYploy new technology, the success of acquisitions and branch expansion, changes in interest rates, and other factors detailed in the Company's SEC filings, including the "Risk Factors" and "Management's Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations" sections of the Company's most recent Form 10--K and Form 10--Q.

Category: Financial

Source: Sierra Bancorp

View source version on businesswire.com: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20260424509241/en/

CONTACT: Kevin McPhaill, President/Chief Executive Officer

Phone: (559) 782-4900 or (888) 454-BANK

Website Address: www.sierrabancorp.com

data.title: Bristol Myers, Pfizer to Offer Discounted Eliquis on Mark Cuban's Cost Plus Drug
data.description:
By Colin Kellaher

Bristol Myers Squibb and Pfizer plan to make the widely used blood thinner Eliquis available to patients at a discounted cash price on Mark Cuban's Cost Plus Drug online pharmacy.

The drugmakers on Friday said Eliquis will be available on Cost Plus Drugs starting next week, with patients with a prescription paying a total of $345 for a 30-day supply.

Bristol Myers and Pfizer, which have a joint venture that markets Eliquis, last year launched a direct-to-consumer service to allow uninsured or underinsured patients to buy a 30-day supply of the drug for $346, more than 40% below the list price of $606 at the time.

The move came after the Trump administration put pressure on the industry to cut drug prices.

Eliquis, one of the top-selling prescription drugs, generated more than $14.4 billion in global sales last year. It is used to prevent blood clots and to ward off strokes in people who have irregular heart rhythms.

-Peter Loftus contributed to this article.

Write to Colin Kellaher at colin.kellaher@wsj.com

data.title: SLB NV reports results for the quarter ended March 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• SLB NV reported quarterly adjusted earnings of 52 cents​​ per share for the quarter ended March 31, lower than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of 72 cents. The mean expectation of twenty five analysts for the quarter was for earnings of 52 cents per share. Wall Street expected results to range from 46 cents to 60 cents per share.

  • Revenue rose 2.7% to $8.72 billion from a year ago; analysts expected $8.65 billion.

  • SLB NV's reported EPS for the quarter was 50 cents​.

  • The company reported quarterly net income of $752 million.

  • SLB NV shares had risen by 6.5% this quarter and gained 42.6% so far this year.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had fallen by about 17.8% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, fourteen analysts negatively revised earnings estimates

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 25 "strong buy" or "buy," 3 "hold" and 1 "sell" or "strong sell." The average consensus recommendation for the oil related services and equipment peer group is also "buy"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for SLB NV is $56.55, about 3.2% above its last closing price of $54.74

This summary was machine generated from LSEG data April 24 at 12:01 p.m. UTC. ​All figures in US dollars unless otherwise stated. data.title: Major shareholder announcement
data.description:
Regulatory release 33/2026

Pursuant to Section 30 of the Danish Capital Markets Act, Better Collective A/S hereby announces that Wealth Fund Partners A/S has notified Better Collective A/S that, as of 20 April 2026, Kapitalforeningen BLS Invest has changed its holding of shares/voting rights as follows:

Holding in Better Collective A/S
Capital and voting rights in Better CollectivePreviouslyAs of 20 April 2026
Kapitalforeningen BLS Invest5.08%4.8347%
Total BLS Capital Fondsmæglerselskab A/S1+10.00%+10.00%

1 Including Kapitalforeningen BLS Invest

As of 20 April 2026, Kapitalforeningen BLS Invest holds 2,840,638 shares in Better Collective A/S, corresponding to 4.8347% of the total share capital and voting rights in Better Collective A/S. The notification relates solely to Kapitalforeningen BLS Invest. The aggregate total holding and voting rights of BLS Capital Fondsmæglerselskab A/S, which includes Kapitalforeningen BLS Invest, remains above the 10% threshold.

About Better Collective

Better Collective owns leading digital sport media, sports betting media and esports communities, with a vision to become the leading digital sports media group. We are on a mission to excite sports fans through engaging content and foster passionate communities worldwide. Better Collective's House of Brands includes; HLTV, FUTBIN, AceOdds, Action Network, Playmaker HQ, The Nation Network, and Bolavip. The company is headquartered in Copenhagen, Denmark, and dual listed on Nasdaq Stockholm (BETCO) and Nasdaq Copenhagen (BETCO DKK). To learn more about Better Collective please visit bettercollective.com.

data.title: Meta Signs Multibillion-Dollar Deal With Amazon to Use Its CPU Chips for AI — WSJ
data.description:
By Belle Lin

Meta Platforms and Amazon.com agreed to a multibillion-dollar deal over several years in which the social-media company will use tens of millions of Amazon Web Services' Graviton chip cores to support its AI agents and other AI initiatives.

The companies declined to disclose the financial terms and exact duration of the deal. Nafea Bshara, an Amazon vice president and distinguished engineer, said the length of the deal is between three and five years.

Bshara, a co-founder of AWS's in-house chip unit Annapurna Labs, said most of the tens of millions of AWS Graviton cores will be located in the U.S.

The news comes as tech giants and artificial intelligence labs are still scrambling to get as much compute capacity as possible to support their AI goals. Meta's deal with AWS is one of several it has announced with chip companies this year, including Nvidia, Advanced Micro Devices and Arm Holdings, and underscores the need for a diversity of chips to support AI, analysts say.

"Meta has, as you can imagine, access to so many options from the supply side. But they chose Graviton5, our 3-nanometer chip, for price performance," Amazon's Bshara said.

Meta signed this agreement for AWS's Graviton central processing units, or CPUs — a category of chip that had largely been left behind as the AI world focused its attention on graphics processing units, or GPUs.

Now, thanks to the rise of AI agents, CPUs are once again becoming a hot commodity. And companies like Intel, which specialize in producing CPUs, are benefiting from the trend.

Meta said the new deal reflects its diversified approach to infrastructure, and shows that no single chip architecture can efficiently serve every computational task.

CPUs are useful for running certain applications and feeding that activity back to GPUs — or in other words, the two chips work well together for a variety of tasks that AI agents can accomplish, said Brendan Burke, a research director at Futurum Group focused on semiconductors.

CPUs are also a key part of the "post-training" step for large language models, or the part of the AI model-building process where pretrained models are more actively trained toward certain goals.

This isn't the first time Meta and AWS have worked together. The partnership between the two tech giants dates back to about 2016, Bshara said, but had mostly involved core cloud services, use of Amazon's Bedrock platform and Meta renting GPU clusters from AWS.

Meta, which agreed to buy the AI startup Manus for more than $2 billion in December, clearly has AI agent ambitions — and those drive demand for CPUs in their data centers, Burke said. Manus develops an AI agent that can carry out sophisticated tasks.

"It's clear that for the largest frontier labs, there is no limit to their demand for CPUs," Burke said.

To streamline its operations and pay for its massive investments in AI, Meta said Thursday that it will lay off 10% of its staff, or roughly 8,000 people, in May. The company released its first new AI model in a year, called Muse Spark, earlier this month, and said it has plans for more releases soon.

For AWS, the deal with Meta serves as a proof point that its homegrown Graviton CPUs are highly useful for powering AI, according to Futurum's Burke. The agreement makes Meta one of AWS's five largest Graviton customers, Bshara said.

Earlier this week, Amazon announced an additional $5 billion investment in Anthropic, which included the AI company using tens of millions of AWS Graviton CPU cores.

AWS has been building its own in-house-designed silicon since well before 2018, when it released the first version of Graviton based on processor architecture from Arm.

But AWS isn't only making bets on its own chips. In March, the company announced a deal with Cerebras where it will deploy the startup's inference chips inside its data centers.

Write to Belle Lin at belle.lin@wsj.com
